similarity_name: Temporal, geographical, and domain validation distance
definition: Represent clinical examples by deployment-relevant metadata such as time period, hospital/site, geography, population, care setting, and phenotype definition. Use exact-match overlap, time distance, or site/domain distance to build SPECTRA curves over increasingly external validation settings.
example_usage: python metadata_distance_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --columns site year care_setting

citations.title: Perspectives on validation of clinical predictive algorithms

evidence.summary: The paper organizes clinical algorithm generalizability into temporal, geographical, and domain types.
The supplementary table gives temporal validation cohorts and external validation across countries, health systems, and hospital types.

limitations: Metadata distance is only meaningful if the chosen context variables reflect the intended deployment setting.
Site labels may proxy for many unmeasured differences including practice patterns and measurement systems.
Temporal splits can confound calendar drift with data pipeline changes.
quality_gates: State the target deployment context before choosing distance variables.
Separate temporal, site/geographic, and domain axes when sample size permits.
Report calibration as well as discrimination across spectral bins.
